2604.10514.title: Data-Efficient Surgical Phase Segmentation in Small-Incision Cataract Surgery: A Controlled Study of Vision Foundation Models
2604.10514.paper_summary: Surgical phase segmentation is central to computer-assisted surgery, yet robust models remain difficult to develop when labeled surgical videos are scarce. We study data-efficient phase segmentation for manual small-incision cataract surgery (SICS) through a controlled comparison of visual representations. To isolate representation quality, we pair each visual encoder with the same temporal model (MS-TCN++) under identical training and evaluation settings on SICS-155 (19 phases). We compare supervised encoders (ResNet-50, I3D) against large self-supervised foundation models (DINOv3, V-JEPA2), and use a cached-feature pipeline that decouples expensive visual encoding from lightweight temporal learning. Foundation-model features improve segmentation performance in this setup, with DINOv3 ViT-7B achieving the best overall results (83.4% accuracy, 87.0 edit score). We further examine cataract-domain transfer using unlabeled videos and lightweight adaptation, and analyze when it helps or hurts. Overall, the study indicates strong transferability of modern vision foundation models to surgical workflow understanding and provides practical guidance for low-label medical video settings. The project website is available at: https://sl2005.github.io/DataEfficient-sics-phase-seg/
2604.10514.paper_summary_zh: 手術階段分割是電腦輔助手術的核心，但在標記手術視頻稀缺的情況下，穩健的模型仍然難以開發。我們通過對視覺表示的控制比較，研究手動小切口白內障手術（SICS）的數據高效階段分割。為了隔離表示質量，我們將每個視覺編碼器與相同的時間模型（MS-TCN++）配對，並在相同的訓練和評估設置下進行（SICS-155，19個階段）。我們比較了監督編碼器（ResNet-50，I3D）與大型自監督基礎模型（DINOv3，V-JEPA2），並使用一個緩存特徵管道，將昂貴的視覺編碼與輕量級的時間學習解耦。在這個設置中，基礎模型特徵提高了分割性能，其中DINOv3 ViT-7B達到了最佳的整體結果（83.4%準確率，87.0編輯分數）。我們進一步檢查了使用未標記視頻和輕量適應的白內障領域轉移，並分析了何時有助於或有害。總體而言，這項研究表明現代視覺基礎模型對手術工作流程理解具有強大的可轉移性，並為低標籤醫療視頻環境提供了實用指導。項目網站可在以下網址訪問：https://sl2005.github.io/DataEfficient-sics-phase-seg/
2604.10514.author: Lincoln Spencer et.al.
2604.10514.authors: Lincoln Spencer, Song Wang, Chen Chen
